extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the sprint after jumping with 500m to go when the peloton hesitated, beating SAGAN who led out too long.
tactical_patterns: Viviani won by attacking 500m out when peloton hesitated, not waiting for traditional leadout sprint positioning.
Quick-Step lost Richeze/Morkov in chaos but Viviani still executed solo jump after using Sagan's wheel through corners.
Technical right-hand kink 500m from finish caused hesitation that allowed Viviani to launch before normal sprint development.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: demonstrated ability to win 226km stage sprint without full leadout train when identifying peloton hesitation
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Viviani noted Sagan appears to have weight loss and body problems despite strong technical positioning
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: present in final sprint positioning but unable to challenge Viviani's early acceleration
